creator: dinoex@FreeBSD.org
text:
I am unable to reproduce the problem yet.

with my setup on FreebSD 11.1 amd64, sendmail+tls+sasl2-8.15.2_8 and openssl-1.0.2n,1
I send and receive mails via TLS.

Also a clean build on FreebSD 11.1 amd64, with ssl from base.
Allows me to deliver a mail with STARTTLS handshake successful.

Also the package from the FreebSD 11.1 amd64 latest repo, using ssl from base.
Allows me to deliver a mail with STARTTLS handshake successful.

What Version of FreeBSD are you using?
What Arch are you using ?
What version of SSL is installed ?

Your LDD output should look like this:

$ ldd /usr/local/sbin/sendmail
/usr/local/sbin/sendmail:
        libwrap.so.6 => /usr/lib/libwrap.so.6 (0x8008d5000)
        libsasl2.so.3 => /usr/local/lib/libsasl2.so.3 (0x800ade000)
        libblacklist.so.0 => /usr/lib/libblacklist.so.0 (0x800cfa000)
        libssl.so.8 => /usr/lib/libssl.so.8 (0x800efd000)
        libcrypto.so.8 => /lib/libcrypto.so.8 (0x801200000)
        libutil.so.9 => /lib/libutil.so.9 (0x801669000)
        libc.so.7 => /lib/libc.so.7 (0x80187d000)
        libthr.so.3 => /lib/libthr.so.3 (0x801c35000)
